refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a top-level KPI requirement that spans multiple system concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (local or remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement clarifies that productivity is a system-level outcome dependent on vehicle performance, teleoperation capability (if used), and safety constraints. The requirement is inherently multi-domain and requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing task execution, measurement, and safety integration.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials over the agreed evaluation period; measure actual productivity (m³/h or ton/h) against target; document compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies; correlate productivity with operator mode (on-site vs. remote) and task type.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed, precision, and power to achieve the productivity target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall provide operator control and situational awareness with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity target performance.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) and operational status via telemetry.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies without degrading productivity below target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-performance heavy equipment platform (vehicle) with autonomous or operator-controlled task execution, coupled with real-time telemetry and safety monitoring to achieve and sustain the productivity target.

DP-B: Deploy a modular architecture where vehicle task execution, teleoperation capability, telemetry collection, and safety enforcement are independently optimized and coordinated via standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link).

DP-C: Implement a closed-loop productivity control system that dynamically adjusts task parameters (speed, load, cycle time) based on real-time telemetry feedback and safety constraints to maintain productivity ≥ target.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Deploy a modular architecture where vehicle task execution, teleoperation capability, telemetry collection, and safety enforcement are independently optimized and coordinated via standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link).

potential_sources: Telemetry interface must carry sufficient fidelity (cycle time, load mass, position) to enable both productivity measurement and safety decision-making; risk of under-specification.
Tasking interface must support both on-site operator commands and remote tele-operator commands; risk of mode-switching latency affecting productivity.
SafetyAlert interface must not introduce blocking delays that degrade productivity; requires careful priority and timeout design.
Network Link reliability and latency are critical for teleoperation mode; degradation directly impacts productivity target.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B is modular and aligns with the multi-stakeholder, multi-domain nature of the requirement. Coupling is managed through explicit interface contracts (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link). Decoupling is achievable if interfaces are well-specified; however, productivity and safety are inherently coupled at the operational level and cannot be fully decoupled—this is a design constraint, not a flaw.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Productivity target is achievable with current heavy equipment technology and operator skill levels.
Assumption: Worksite safety plan constraints do not fundamentally conflict with productivity target (i.e., safety measures are compatible with target throughput).
Risk: If teleoperation latency exceeds operator reaction time, productivity will degrade; requires Network Link specification and validation.
Risk: If telemetry fidelity is insufficient, productivity measurement will be inaccurate, making it impossible to verify compliance with target.
Risk: If safety enforcement is overly conservative, it may block task execution and prevent reaching productivity target.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly specify: (1) Telemetry interface data rate, latency, and accuracy requirements; (2) Tasking interface command latency and mode-switching protocol; (3) SafetyAlert interface priority levels and timeout behavior; (4) Network Link SLA (availability, latency, jitter) for teleoperation mode. These specifications are prerequisites for decomposing into sub-DPs at the next level.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. It owns the overall productivity KPI, system-level interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link), and the integration logic that ensures all subsystems work together to achieve the productivity target while maintaining safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h), vehicle status (position, load, cycle time), and operational health data to external monitoring systems (Site Manager dashboard, Maintenance systems).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task commands from on-site Operator or Tele-operator (via Teleoperation subsystem), specifying dig location, load target, dump location, and priority.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives safety constraints and alerts from Safety Services (e.g., geofence violations, personnel proximity warnings) and sends operational status to Safety Services for real-time safety decision-making.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Teleoperation subsystem (command/feedback loop) and external monitoring/control systems when in remote operation mode.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the productivity KPI requirement. It owns system integration, interface orchestration, and overall KPI accountability. Minimal diffusion: only the actual task execution logic (dig, load, dump) is delegated to Vehicle subsystem; teleoperation and safety are delegated to their respective subsystems but coordinated by NGHE Solution.

Vehicle owns task execution (dig, load, dump) and contributes significantly to productivity. However, Vehicle alone cannot satisfy the productivity KPI because it lacks teleoperation capability, telemetry aggregation, and safety integration. Assigning the full FR to Vehicle would diffuse responsibility for teleoperation latency, safety constraints, and measurement accuracy.

Teleoperation is critical for remote operator productivity but does not own vehicle task execution or safety enforcement. Assigning the full FR to Teleoperation would create diffusion across Vehicle and Safety Services.

Safety Services enforces constraints that affect productivity but does not own task execution or teleoperation. Assigning the full FR to Safety Services would diffuse responsibility for vehicle performance and operator interface.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate owner of CR-NGHE-001 because: (1) it is the only module that spans all contributing subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (2) it owns the system-level KPI accountability; (3) it orchestrates the interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) that enable productivity measurement and safety-aware task execution; (4) it can decompose the requirement into sub-FRs that are then allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services with clear ownership boundaries.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution is treated as a passive container rather than an active orchestrator, integration logic may be diffused across subsystems, leading to unclear accountability for productivity KPI.

Risk: If interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are not rigorously specified at the NGHE Solution level, subsystems may implement incompatible versions, causing integration failures.

Risk: If NGHE Solution does not explicitly model the coupling between productivity and safety, safety constraints may be applied reactively (after productivity degradation) rather than proactively (during task planning).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish NGHE Solution as the owner of CR-NGHE-001 with explicit responsibilities: (1) define and maintain the Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interface specifications; (2) implement the integration logic that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services; (3) own the productivity KPI measurement and reporting; (4) allocate sub-FRs to Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (remote control), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement) with clear success criteria for each. Document the allocation in a traceability matrix.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR clearly states the productivity target, evaluation period, and safety compliance constraints. DP-B (modular architecture with standardized interfaces) directly addresses the FR by enabling independent optimization of task execution, teleoperation, and safety while maintaining system-level coordination. The DP is neither over-constrained nor under-specified; it provides sufficient guidance for decomposition into sub-DPs without prescribing implementation details (e.g., specific vehicle model, teleoperation protocol).

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: If interface specifications (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are not owned by NGHE Solution, subsystems may implement incompatible versions, causing integration failures and productivity loss.
Diffusion Risk 2: If telemetry collection is distributed across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems without a central aggregator, productivity measurement may be inconsistent or incomplete.
Diffusion Risk 3: If safety enforcement logic is split between Safety Services and Vehicle (e.g., geofence checks in both), there is risk of conflicting decisions affecting productivity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary because it owns the interface contracts and integration logic. However, the actual implementation of NGHE Solution must be carefully designed to avoid diffusion. Recommend: (1) NGHE Solution owns interface specifications and orchestration logic; (2) Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services implement their respective sub-DPs and expose only the specified interfaces; (3) integration testing validates that all subsystems conform to interface contracts and that the system achieves the productivity target.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 is feasible through representative operations trials: (1) measure actual productivity (m³/h, ton/h) via Telemetry interface; (2) verify compliance with worksite safety plan via SafetyAlert interface and operational logs; (3) correlate productivity with operator mode (on-site vs. remote) and task type. Success criteria: productivity ≥ target over the agreed evaluation period, zero safety plan violations.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear. Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (remote control), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Each sub-FR has a clear owner and success criteria. Traceability from CR-NGHE-001 to sub-FRs to sub-DPs to sub-IMs is established.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Specify the Telemetry interface in detail: data elements (cycle time, load mass, position, fuel consumption), sampling rate, latency, and accuracy. This is critical for productivity measurement and safety decision-making.

Revision 2: Specify the Tasking interface: command types (dig, load, dump, move), parameters (location, load target), latency requirements, and mode-switching protocol (on-site to remote). This is critical for operator control and teleoperation.

Revision 3: Specify the SafetyAlert interface: alert types (geofence violation, personnel proximity, equipment fault), priority levels, and timeout behavior. This is critical for safety-aware task execution.
Revision 4: Specify the Network Link SLA for teleoperation mode: availability (%), latency (ms), jitter (ms), and bandwidth (Mbps). This is critical for remote operator productivity.

Revision 5: Define the productivity target in detail: m³/h or ton/h, task type (dig, load, dump, or cycle), soil/material type, equipment configuration, and evaluation period. This is critical for verification.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a top-level KPI requirement that is not atomic and requires decomposition into sub-FRs addressing task execution, teleoperation, telemetry, and safety integration. The refined FR, selected DP (modular architecture), and assigned IM (NGHE Solution) are sound and pass all gates. However, the requirement is too broad to be implemented at a single level; it must be decomposed into sub-FRs that can be allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to establish clear ownership and success criteria for each subsystem.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR identifies four sub-FRs: (1) vehicle task execution, (2) teleoperation capability, (3) telemetry measurement, and (4) safety constraint enforcement. Each sub-FR is a candidate for the next decomposition level. The selected DP (modular architecture with standardized interfaces) provides the framework for decomposing these sub-FRs into sub-DPs and allocating them to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the appropriate owner at this level, but it must delegate to sub-IMs at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with sufficient speed, precision, and power to achieve the productivity target.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall provide operator control and situational awareness with latency and fidelity sufficient to maintain productivity target performance.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and report productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h) and operational status via the Telemetry interface.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The safety services subsystem shall enforce worksite safety plan constraints and operational policies without degrading productivity below target.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Productivity target is achievable with current heavy equipment technology and operator skill levels.
Assumption 2: Worksite safety plan constraints are compatible with productivity target (i.e., safety measures do not fundamentally prevent reaching target throughput).
Assumption 3: Teleoperation latency can be managed to within operator reaction time (typically 200-500 ms for heavy equipment).
Assumption 4: Telemetry fidelity (cycle time, load mass, position) is sufficient to measure productivity accurately (±5% or better).
Assumption 5: Safety enforcement can be implemented without blocking task execution (i.e., safety constraints are enforced proactively during task planning, not reactively after violations).
Assumption 6: NGHE Solution can be implemented as an active orchestrator (not a passive container) that coordinates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems.
open_risks: Risk 1: Teleoperation latency may exceed operator reaction time, degrading productivity. Mitigation: specify Network Link SLA and validate with operator trials.
Risk 2: Telemetry fidelity may be insufficient for accurate productivity measurement. Mitigation: specify Telemetry interface in detail and validate with field data.
Risk 3: Safety constraints may be overly conservative, blocking task execution and preventing productivity target. Mitigation: involve Safety Manager in DP selection and validate with safety trials.

Risk 4: Interface specifications (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) may be incompatible across subsystems, causing integration failures. Mitigation: establish interface governance and conduct integration testing.

Risk 5: NGHE Solution may be implemented as a passive container, diffusing integration logic across subsystems. Mitigation: establish clear ownership of integration logic and conduct architecture reviews.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Productivity target specification (m³/h or ton/h, task type, soil/material type, equipment configuration, evaluation period).
Evidence 2: Worksite safety plan and operational policies (geofence constraints, personnel proximity rules, equipment fault handling).
Evidence 3: Telemetry interface specification (data elements, sampling rate, latency, accuracy).
Evidence 4: Tasking interface specification (command types, parameters, latency, mode-switching protocol).
Evidence 5: SafetyAlert interface specification (alert types, priority levels, timeout behavior).
Evidence 6: Network Link SLA for teleoperation mode (availability, latency, jitter, bandwidth).
Evidence 7: Operator reaction time data for heavy equipment (to validate teleoperation latency requirements).
Evidence 8: Representative operations trial plan (task types, evaluation period, measurement methodology, success criteria).
